Title: Advances in functional studies of plant MYC transcription factors

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy reserve supporting pollen germination and maturation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In pollen grains, anther walls, and ovaries during flower development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In pollen grains, anther walls, and ovaries during flower development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation and starch-to-sugar transition during flower development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtMYC2a regulates genes associated with starch metabolism including AGP, SS2, and BAM1 during flower development. 2. Overexpression of NtMYC2a significantly increased starch content and accelerated pollen germination and maturation. 3. Silencing of NtMYC2a significantly decreased starch content in pollen grains.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; gene silencing; starch content measurement; expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve pollen vigor and reproductive performance via carbohydrate metabolism regulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a metabolism-related MYC-regulated non-enzyme trait in a review context.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and defense signaling support
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by brown planthopper feeding via OsMYC2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases mixed-linkage glucan deposition, reinforcing cell wall thickness and defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYC2 directly binds the OsCslF6 promoter upon brown planthopper feeding. 2. OsCslF6 produces mixed-linkage glucans that reinforce vascular bundle cell walls. 3. The reinforced wall stimulates OsLecRK1-mediated defense signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding evidence; herbivore induction analysis; phenotypic defense assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving structural defense against sap-feeding insects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because primary role is wall polysaccharide deposition/defense rather than classical nutrient quality pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the rate-limiting irreversible oxidative decarboxylation of indole-3-pyruvate acid to produce indole-3-acetic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated growth and wound response modulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA-induced auxin biosynthesis associated with wound defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC8 is induced by JA through MYC2/3/4 binding to its G-box-containing promoter. 2. It participates in wound-induced auxin biosynthesis. 3. Its expression magnitude depends strongly on MYC2 abundance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning wound-induced auxin-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed together with YUC9 as major MYC targets in auxin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the rate-limiting irreversible oxidative decarboxylation of indole-3-pyruvate acid to produce indole-3-acetic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated growth and wound response modulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes wound-induced auxin accumulation and spider-mite resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC9 is induced by JA via MYC2/3/4. 2. Expression is strongly reduced in myc2 knockout mutants. 3. Overexpression of YUC9 increased resistance to spider mites, linking auxin biosynthesis with herbivore defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout expression analysis; overexpression; herbivore resistance assay; promoter binding
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mechanical damage; JA induction; spider mite feeding
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering wound-induced resistance to mites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Resistance mechanism may involve downstream ethylene/lignin effects rather than auxin alone.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates putrescine to form N-methylputrescine in the nicotine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased PMT2 expression promotes high nicotine production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtMYC2 binds the G-box in the PMT2 promoter to activate transcription. 2. ERF189 and ERF221 collaborate with NtMYC2 for maximal transactivation. 3. PMT2 is a central MYC-controlled step in nicotine biosynthesis under JA signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding; transactivation assays; elicitor-responsive expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter with G-box and GCC-box
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key engineering target for nicotine-related defense metabolite enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Collaborative regulation with NIC2-locus ERFs is emphasized.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to pyridine-ring precursor formation used in nicotine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher QPT2 expression supports enhanced nicotine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF189 and ERF221 recognize GCC-boxes in QPT2 proximal promoters. 2. NtMYC2 collaborates with ERFs to maximize transactivation of nicotine pathway genes including QPT2. 3. QPT2 functions in supplying precursor for nicotine production under JA signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: QPRT transcript levels increased after MeJA elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QPRT transcript levels increased after MeJA elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply node for nicotine engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper uses both QPT2 and QPRT wording across cited studies.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates ornithine to form putrescine used for phenylpropanoid-polyamine conjugate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites against insects and pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of ODC by SlMYC1/2 increases PPC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC1 and SlMYC2 redundantly increase PPC biosynthesis by up-regulating ODC expression. 2. ODC supplies putrescine precursor for PPC formation. 3. This contributes to defense-associated specialized metabolism in Solanaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression regulation evidence in cited functional studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential entry point to elevate defensive PPC accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Enzyme itself is not the review's primary focus but is a summarized MYC metabolic target.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Desaturated sterol intermediate for SGA formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a C5(6) double bond into sterol intermediates used in steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of C5-SD2 promotes constitutive SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC1/2 increase constitutive SGA biosynthesis by up-regulating C5-SD2. 2. SlMYC1/2 act synergistically with GAME9 to transactivate the C5-SD2 promoter. 3. JAZ competition with GAME9 modulates the magnitude of SGA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with suppression of GA-mediated growth under stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for SGA engineering, with caution due to toxicity/growth tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central structural target in MYC-GAME9 synergistic control.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranyl diphosphate into the monoterpene volatile linalool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect defense by attracting natural enemies and inducing defense-related genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes linalool emission for herbivore defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RERJ1 specifically induces OsLIS expression after wounding. 2. OsMYC2 also regulates OsLIS-associated defense outputs. 3. Linalool emission contributes to indirect anti-herbivore defense and defense gene induction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Wounding induction analysis; expression studies; protein interaction context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential resource allocation cost to volatile defense
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for boosting indirect herbivore resistance via volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as MYC-associated target in rice defense metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of sesquiterpene volatile(s) in Arabidopsis inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYC2 binds the AtTPS11 promoter to activate expression. 2. Full induction requires JA-activated MYC2 and GA-mediated DELLA removal. 3. The gene contributes to Arabidopsis terpene defense metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Environmental cues; JA signaling; GA developmental program
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for tuning volatile terpene-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed with AtTPS21 as MYC2 direct targets.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of sesquiterpene volatile(s) in Arabidopsis inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYC2 binds the AtTPS21 promoter to activate expression. 2. Complete induction requires JA signaling and GA-induced DELLA degradation. 3. MYC2 can cooperate with MYB21 for terpene alcohol/linalool-related promoter activation in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Environmental cues; JA signaling; GA developmental program
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering terpene-based ecological defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Frequently discussed together with AtTPS11 in MYC2 terpene regulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to taxadiene, the committed precursor of paclitaxel
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer diterpenoid and plant defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of TASY promotes paclitaxel biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcMYC2a directly binds the TASY promoter or indirectly regulates it through ERF12/15. 2. MYC2/3/4 in Taxus media activate paclitaxel biosynthetic genes via MED25 recruitment. 3. JAMs repress paclitaxel synthesis genes through E-box binding, indicating tight antagonistic control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding; overexpression; transient expression; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA or coronatine treatment; salt and drought stress in cited Taxus studies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential excessive JA sensitivity and growth inhibition if overactivated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime engineering target for paclitaxel production systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Different Taxus species/studies reported activating versus repressive MYC homolog effects depending on species and timing.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tryptophan to produce tryptamine for strictosidine and downstream TIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites and source of anticancer compounds vincristine/vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Various tissues; pathway branch differs by tissue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CrMYC2 activation of TDC promotes TIA pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2 directly transactivates TDC by binding its promoter. 2. GBF1/2 and ZCT repressors modulate TDC promoter activity downstream of CrMYC2 networks. 3. TDC is a major MYC-controlled entry point into the indole branch of TIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation; binding assays; overexpression studies
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrMYC2; GBF1; GBF2; ZCT1; ZCT2; ZCT3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed broadly, CrMYC2 preferentially in root
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA and ET treatment; developmental context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression can up-regulate repressors and reduce pathway output in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important leverage point for TIA engineering, but requires balancing repressor circuits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes complex feedback around CrMYC2-TDC-ORCA control.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant, UV protection, stress tolerance, pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulated DFR expression enhances anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of MYC2, MYC3, or MYC4 increased anthocyanin accumulation with concomitant DFR up-regulation. 2. DFR is a major late biosynthetic target in MYC-mediated anthocyanin induction. 3. Additional species such as Gynura bicolor show MYC/MYB cooperation on DFR promoters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression analysis; anthocyanin quantification; promoter regulation evidence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA; light quality/intensity; phosphate deficiency; stress
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Resource diversion from growth and lignin branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural marker/target for boosting anthocyanin-rich tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because repeatedly summarized as core structural metabolic target of MYCs.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor, subgroup IIIe; contains JID, TAD, and bHLH domains
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: multiple nutrient/stress-related metabolites including glucosinolates, anthocyanins, terpenes, nicotine, auxin, steroidal glycoalkaloids, phenylpropanoid-polyamine conjugates
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: multiple classes: glucosinolate; flavonoid; terpenoid; alkaloid; hormone
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and repression; protein-protein interaction; feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and transcriptional reprogramming of specialized metabolites; precursor supply; branch regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ genes; GS biosynthesis genes including CYP79B3 SOT16 IPMDH1 BCAT4; YUC8; YUC9; PMT2; QPT2; C5-SD2; ODC; AtTPS11; AtTPS21; TASY; TDC; PAP1/MYB75; DFR; SAG29; PAO; ACS1; ACO1; TOR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates many pathway genes and can repress selected branches depending on context, e.g. represses pathogen/ET branch genes and can negatively affect indolic glucosinolate branch in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 is the central MYC-family regulator repeatedly summarized as a master switch integrating JA, light, and other hormone cues to control secondary and primary metabolism. 2. It directly activates multiple biosynthetic genes for glucosinolates, auxin, nicotine, terpenes, anthocyanins and other metabolites across species. 3. MYC2 also participates in feedback loops through induction of JAZs, JAMs, and other repressors, fine-tuning metabolic amplitude and duration.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoters with G-box/E-box motifs; transgenic overexpression promoters not consistently specified in this review
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generally promotes accumulation of many defense-associated metabolites, while some branches/species show repression or context dependence
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: MED25 recruits HAC1 and RNA polymerase II to MYC2 target promoters; JAZ-associated HDAC repression reported in JA/ET contexts
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA/MeJA; wounding; herbivory; pathogen infection; light quality and intensity; phosphate starvation; drought; cold; salt; ABA; GA
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Review cites transcriptomic, proteomic, ChIP-seq and other omics studies; no single accession reported in review text
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator and molecular switch of JA-dependent metabolic reprogramming across diverse nutrient/defense metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; overexpression; RNAi/silencing; promoter binding assays; ChIP/yeast one-hybrid; transient transactivation; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZs; MED25; MYC3; MYC4; MYC5; DELLAs; EIN3; EIL1; PIF3; PIF4; HY5; GAME9; MYB21; PAP1/MYB75; RERJ1; ERF189; ERF221; PYL6; TIC; FHY3; FAR1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate/JA-Ile; COI1; light/PHYB/PHYA/CRYs; ABA; GA; ET; SA; Pi starvation; drought; cold; salt; circadian clock
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Strong negative feedback via activation of JAZs and JAMs; positive loops with Dof2.1 and RGL3 in certain contexts
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, inflorescences, roots, fruits, pollen and species-specific defense tissues depending on metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth inhibition; reduced seed yield/fertility; altered flowering; stress-defense growth tradeoff
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homo/heterodimers with MYC3/4; complexes with MED25, JAZs, MYBs, PIFs and other regulators
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value hub for engineering defense metabolites and stress resilience, but requires careful tuning due to strong pleiotropy and growth penalties
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis and many homologs in other plant species
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana and homologs across angiosperms
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed; inducible by JA and many stresses; stability strongly light dependent
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores/pathogens; antioxidant and stress adaptation; ecological signaling; some human nutritional/pharmaceutical value
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review paper; MYC2 is the principal summarized regulator relevant to metabolism across species and conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glucosinolates; anthocyanins; terpenes and other JA-induced metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; dimerization with other MYCs; feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ genes; GS pathway genes; YUC8/9; DFR; HY5-related anthocyanin module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates many JA-responsive metabolic genes; often stronger positive role than MYC2 for some branches such as indolic glucosinolate/tryptophan-derived pathways
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC3 acts redundantly with MYC2/4/5 in many JA-regulated metabolic outputs. 2. It is highlighted as a major positive regulator of tryptophan and indolic glucosinolate biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. 3. It contributes to anthocyanin and terpene regulation and participates in JAZ-mediated feedback circuits.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of several JA-induced defense metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive contributor within redundant MYC module, especially for indolic glucosinolate-related metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant combinations; overexpression; promoter binding; transcriptomics
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Activates JAZ genes in negative feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with other MYCs for boosting defense metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review treats MYC2/3/4/5 as overlapping regulators but MYC3 is emphasized for certain biosynthetic branches.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glucosinolates; anthocyanins; terpenes and other JA-induced metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/repression depending on branch; dimerization with MYC2/3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-mediated specialized metabolite biosynthesis and branch balance
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ genes; GS genes; DFR; HY5-related module; ET/JA branch genes such as PDF1.2 contextually repressed
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates many wound/defense metabolism genes but can negatively affect ET/JA pathogen branch genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC4 functions redundantly with MYC2/3/5 in JA metabolic regulation. 2. It contributes positively to glucosinolate, anthocyanin and terpene-related outputs. 3. Like MYC2, it can negatively regulate some pathogen-associated JA/ET branch genes, showing branch-specific control.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes several JA-induced metabolite pathways with context-specific repression of alternative branches
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA/MeJA; wounding; herbivory; light; salt-related tSPM context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important redundant MYC component shaping branch specificity of JA metabolic responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; mutant studies; promoter binding; transcriptome analysis
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZs; MED25; MYC2; MYC3; EIN3; EIL1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of combinatorial engineering of JA-regulated metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in review as part of functionally redundant MYC module and sometimes salt-associated via MYC4 expression induction.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, largely redundant with MYC2/3/4
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ genes; HY5-related module; anthocyanin-associated genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC5 acts redundantly with MYC2/3/4 in multiple JA-regulated processes. 2. It contributes to anthocyanin accumulation and defense-associated metabolism. 3. Unlike MYC2/3/4, its expression is not JA-inducible in some senescence contexts, yet it still promotes JA outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports accumulation of JA-regulated defense metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive redundant regulator rather than sole master switch
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely participates in JAZ feedback induction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of combinatorial MYC stacking rather than alone
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broad but context-dependent; not JA-induced in some senescence cases
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review explicitly treats MYC2/3/4/5 as a core redundant regulatory set controlling metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid/anthocyanin-associated regulation and developmental nutrient-associated processes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GL2 and flavonoid/anthocyanin-related modules in broader literature context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulator in some contexts; can destabilize GL1-GL3-TTG1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC1 is distinguished from MYC2/3/4/5 as a IIIf clade bHLH. 2. The review notes that both IIIe and IIIf clades are associated with flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis. 3. MYC1 is also implicated in iron homeostasis, although the present review does not detail direct nutrient-metabolite experiments for it.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with flavonoid/anthocyanin-related regulation in broader review context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Secondary summarized regulator, not main master switch in this review
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associates with TTG1 and affects GL1-GL3-TTG1 complex dynamics
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential ancillary target for flavonoid engineering
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and stress-related antioxidant roles for flavonoids/anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included conservatively because the review explicitly links MYC1 clade to flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis, though not as the main metabolism focus.
